Is that the plan?
I know everyone's life changes, but, you know, I'll just, I'll just share that like, you know, personally, like it has been a little disruptive to.

I understand completely.
Yeah, I understand.

know.

Okay.
Okay.

when you get Ashur's feedback specifically about the product, I know this has been kind of an ongoing issue we've had with, like, making sure that the copy truly reflects how our product works and the way we're messaging it.

Okay.
Yeah.
Send one over.

So, obviously, in December, we had...
Before move on to the performance, Dan, are you just going to assign out all open items?
The remaining, yeah, I will.
Yeah, just assign everything.

Because if it's not assigned to me, I'm not doing it.
I'm happy to do I'm happy to do more.

Carrie, I think we can build an Airtable automation into Slack, right?
With, we can assign somebody, and then, yeah.
Just because, like, I don't get notified when sometimes when it's there.
I realize the, and also when I'm assigning, it, like, produces, like, a Slack, you know, chirp for every single one, which is fine, but.
Yeah.
Okay.
Yeah, well, that would be helpful if I could just get, I think I need to be notified when something new is in there, and then I'll just get in there and manually assign.
I assigned a bunch of Apurva, but I'm going to get the rest out.
Yeah, think what we can do is when something moves into under review, then we could just pick you in Slack.
Yeah, that would be helpful.
Yep.
We can either do like a digest or every, do you want them into, I'm assuming you don't want them individually, like we could just do like a daily or weekly.
Yeah, like.
Get a little excessive if we did every single one.
Right.
Yeah, that's fine.
Just, yeah, just give me like a heads up.
Hey, there's some new stuff in there.
Yeah.
All right.
So, yeah, regarding performance, so far in January, things are looking quite promising.
We seem to be on pace to go ahead of December, so things are definitely picking up.

Things are looking up over there as well.
In terms of our cohorts, non-human identity and identity federation, those are the top performers.
Of course, identity federation historically has been the best performer since we've had this little boost.
Although non-human identity over the past couple of weeks has been seeing a nice little boost over there.
Moving on to the LLM referral side of things.
LLM referral traffic, obviously, it dropped in December given the holidays.
But as you can see for January, we're almost catching up over a couple of weeks, all of December's traffic.
So I'm anticipating a pretty good result over there by the end of the month.
And within Scrunch, the reason I don't have a screenshot over here is there seems to be some kind of a data issue with the Aembit account.
They changed a few definitions of benchmarks within Scrunch to the way they measure brand presence.
So because of that, some of those charts have gone a bit wonky.
And there is also an internal growthx tool that we are planning on rolling out to our clients.
Gary, do you want to just talk about, check that a little bit?
Because I know you've been working on that quite extensively.
Yeah, I can do that.
Check That is our own proprietary AI visibility tool.

So on the surface, it looks quite a bit like Scrunch, but the main difference really there is going to be like what Scrunch, I think even Profound, Peak, all those, they require you to kind of like populate your own and build your own tracking dimensions, right?
Like which prompts you're going to track.
We actually start with a library based on some categorization things that we've done.
And so you start with a library of, you know, hundreds of prompts, and then you can add your custom ones on there.
And they're mostly, almost exclusively actually, centered around evaluation stage prompts.
So the higher intent things, and for that reason, you see a lot more branded mentions and a lot more citations.
But it's a little more accurate view because

Those are the kinds of questions that people mostly go to LLMs for, right, is they're trying to decide between two tools, or they're trying to learn more about a specific tool, not necessarily to say, you know, what is insert job to be done here, you know what I mean?
They're not necessarily asking those questions of AI, so that's kind of the starting point of it.
I do not think, I'm pretty sure that your workspace isn't built out yet, but I can give you, like, a full demo of it next week, if you would like.
But that's what we'll be using behind the scenes to track LLM performance, so it'll be a more accurate than the refers breakdown that we normally give.
Okay, I know we just approved some stuff that is LLM specific, right?
Yes, got it.
Yep.
So, yeah, that's pretty much it, and again,

Again, I just want to, you know, I appreciate that this seems like, this can be a little disruptive with me transitioning off and, you know, but I can assure you that we've done a lot of prep work in the background to minimize, you know, any disruption with a very conscious of how things have been, etc.
So I can assure you that, you know, you're at great hands with Carrie and I am around next week as well.
So, you know, the transition is going to go smoothly.

So, yeah, that was pretty much all I had for you today.
All right.
We'll, we'll get working on the, the stuff for review.
And Etisham is still our, the person who's staging everything?
Correct.
Yes.
Thanks
Okay.
Okay.
It might be, I have a couple comments from me and then from our designer regarding some of the staging work.
So maybe, and then just because he's familiar with the account, you won't be on next week's, Vivek?
You're done?
No, I'm here next week as well.
Okay.
Well, maybe you can invite him if you don't mind.
Or I can connect with him separately, but I don't know.
I'm just wondering.
He seems to know our account well, right?
So.
Yeah.
If you have any feedback in the meantime to pass on, I can pass that so that, you know, he has enough context playing it.
Okay.
Yeah, I'm not looking to blindside him.
I'm just, I'm just wondering if it's even helpful from a continuity perspective to keep him as you guys hand off, just because he seems to, he had been running the show for a while.
But I don't know.
Okay.
But yeah, I do have some personal feedback for him and the work on the back end.
So I'll get that to you, Vivek.
Thank you.
Okay.
Sounds good.
I've worked, this is the third agency I've worked with Edisham at.
So, you know, we're...
Yeah, know.
I love working with Edisham.
He's really great.

So...
Oh, good.
That's good to hear.
Okay.
Yeah.
Okay.
All right.
Thank you, everyone.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Transition account lead and sync on content and performance.](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=297.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Lead Transition:** Carrie Chowske is now the GrowthX account lead, taking over from Vivek Shankar.](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=297.0)
  - [**Content Backlog:** A 51-item review backlog (mostly glossary pages) requires immediate attention from the Aembit team.](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=450.0)
  - [**Product Accuracy:** Content must be more concise and product-accurate; GrowthX will create a "pitfalls" guide to improve quality.](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=667.0)
  - [**Performance Momentum:** January SEO and LLM referral traffic are significantly outpacing December, validating the new top-funnel strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=922.0)

## Topics

### Account Lead Transition

  - [Vivek Shankar is transitioning out of GrowthX; Carrie Chowske is the new account lead.](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=297.0)
  - [Dan Kaplan noted this is the latest in a series of lead changes, which disrupts account continuity.](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=370.0)
  - [Carrie confirmed she is fully briefed on the strategy and content needs.](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=303.0)

### Content Review & Workflow

  - [**Backlog:** 51 items are in the Airtable "Client Review" queue.](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=450.0)
      - [**Priority:** New top-funnel articles.](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=509.0)
      - [**Bulk:** Glossary pages.](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=509.0)
      - [**Deferred:** 14 older articles (MCP topics) are paused for later rewrite.](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=552.0)
  - [**Feedback (Ashur):**](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=570.0)
      - [**Product Accuracy:** The "How Aembit Helps" section is inconsistent.](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=600.0)
          - [**Action:** Remove sections that don't fit; for relevant terms (e.g., OIDC), provide specific guidance and link to Aembit docs.](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=600.0)
      - [**Conciseness:** Content is often repetitive and excessively long, especially in the "pitfalls" section.](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=709.0)
  - [**Process Improvement:**](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=810.0)
      - [**Problem:** Content sits unassigned in Airtable, creating review delays.](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=810.0)
      - [**Solution:** GrowthX will build an Airtable automation to send Dan a daily Slack digest of new items needing assignment.](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=810.0)

### Performance Update

  - [**Overall:** January performance is strong, on pace to exceed December metrics.](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=922.0)
  - [**SEO:** Non-branded impressions are up, validating the new top-funnel strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=922.0)
      - [**Top Performers:** "Non-human identity" and "identity federation."](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=945.0)
  - [**LLM Referrals:** Traffic is recovering quickly from the holiday dip, nearly matching all of December's volume in two weeks.](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=966.0)
  - [**Reporting Tool:** GrowthX will replace Scrunch with a proprietary tool, "Check That."](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=985.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Scrunch data is currently unreliable due to recent benchmark definition changes.](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=985.0)
      - [**Function:** Tracks evaluation-stage prompts for higher-intent, more accurate AI visibility data.](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=1023.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Dan Kaplan:** Assign all remaining items in the Airtable review queue.](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=810.0)
  - [**Carrie Chowske:**](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=709.0)
      - [Send Dan a "pitfalls" guide example to improve content accuracy.](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=709.0)
      - [Build the Airtable-to-Slack automation for review assignments.](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=810.0)
      - [Demo the "Check That" tool next week.](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=1005.0)
  - [**Vivek Shankar:**](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=1206.0)
      - [Invite Etisham (stager) to next week's meeting for feedback continuity.](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=1206.0)
      - [Relay Dan's feedback on staging work to Etisham.](https://fathom.video/share/_z2FEdy1P2ZdjTDEsGeyyYqwdGzz9tqE?tab=summary&timestamp=1206.0)

action_items.description: Share eclipse-viewing instructional materials w/ Vivek
Review remaining Airtable glossary/TF articles; assign unassigned to Ashur/Apurva
Address Ashur’s product-accuracy comments; update glossary 'How Aembit helps' per docs
Send product-messaging pitfalls/guidance examples to Ashur; then create artifact
Implement Airtable→Slack digest for 'Under review' to Dan
Schedule Check That demo w/ Dan next week
Invite Ehtisham to next week’s sync
Send staging feedback (yours + designer’s) to Vivek for Ehtisham
